title: Generics, Type Sets, Constraints, Inference, and Iteration · Program Defense
summary: A collection library needs reusable operations without reflection, lost relationships, over-broad constraints, or unreadable APIs. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a constrained reusable collection library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.
Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Iterators Self Constraints 126
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A collection helper relates key type K to value type V for 7 records. Compare concrete, interface, generic, and reflection designs; constrain only required operations and identify the Go 1.26 feature that the browser subset cannot certify.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Iterators Self Constraints 126.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use range-over-function iteration and evaluate go 1.26 self-referential constraints while preserving cancellation and readability. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Iterators Self Constraints 126, keep Iterators Self Constraints 126 connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
